item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah! Ok, that makes sense. I see it now :).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You might miss it: the final set of logs are key.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In a way, yes. In reality,

+ show block
– hide block

They're all the same person. Smokey is Tezh at different points in time. Note the Foundation presence, as well as the direction he runs. Smokey 'cheats' because he's a young kid: willing to do anything possible. In reality, Tezh caused his own injury: by bumping Smokey, he caused his own injury to himself in the past. As such, when he realizes it, he heads back to become Tait: if you look at all of Tait's notes, they either prevent Tezh from running, or minimize his impact, trying to stop the injury from occurring in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, I think I get it.
– hide block

Smokey is a manifestation of the champion runner that Charles Tezh would have become if not for the injury, and Charles is obsessed with becoming as good of a runner as he would have been without the injury, hence needing to beat Smokey in a race.

After going back in time and staying there, he had to create a new identity, and ended up with the name Tait, and eventually became a senior Foundation researcher.

Smokey cheats because Charles sees the injury as being horrifically unfair.

Am I close?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a (partial) guess...
– hide block

Wait, so Tezh, Tait, and "Smokey" are all the same person? Somehow? And their split has something to do with the knee injury? And their obsession with beating (themselves?) in a race?

Or am I entirely off-base?

I'm still working on figuring this one out :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted despite not really having a clue what's going on here.

item.title: Re: Ironic, Isn't it?
item.content:encoded: I'm happy I could help you out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts as well. Great concept, execution needs work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea, but the tone and pacing are both weak/inconsistent throughout. Novote.

item.title: Re: Ironic, Isn't it?
item.content:encoded: I’m glad that you got this article out of the ideas we tossed around. Good for you Oak, and good luck if you want to write more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice! Not totally sure what's going on in here but I like it. (if you want some nicer image formatting, by the way, stick this in the page where you've got the image code now)

 [[include component:image-block name=image url here|caption=caption goes here]]

Also, you should host the image locally - you can do that in the "files" link at the bottom of the page.

item.title: Ironic, Isn't it?
item.content:encoded:
I'm you: you are me and we are you
I'm you: you are me and we are you
I'm you: you are me and we are you
Estoy alcanzando
Muy profundo adentro
Profundo en mi
Donde ella vivir

First SCP! Thanks for the help, guys. Anyone who did help crit, which would've been, like, 12 people on the SCP Declassified Discord alone.

Hippo, Cimm, Brewster: Thanks for helping me with the idea's creation. This SCP wouldn't be here without you.

KnightAlaska, Em, Rounder, Rock, Gecko and numerous others: Thanks for your help critting. I'll keep your advice at heart.

Perhaps most importantly: Thank you Rura. For giving me the power to keep writing when I felt I could write no longer.

Catch you in the temporal displacement, guys.
